claims.claimText: Agroforestry occupies approximately 8 percent of India's geographical land area.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Agroforestry occupies approximately 8% of India's geographical land area.
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on Indian agriculture and land use statistics.
distractorHooks: Exaggerated coverage such as 20 percent or 30 percent
Understated coverage such as 1 percent

claims.claimText: In agroforestry systems, Agrisilviculture combines agricultural crops with tree crops, whereas Silvi-pasture integrates livestock, forage, and trees.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Agroforestry systems include Agrisilviculture (combines crops with tree crops) and Silvi-pasture (integrates livestock, forage, and trees).
claims.examUse: Correct-pair or match-the-following question on types of agroforestry systems.
distractorHooks: Defining Agrisilviculture as the integration of livestock

claims.claimText: India's pledge under the Bonn Challenge included restoring 13 million hectares of degraded and deforested land by the year 2020.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: India joined the Bonn Challenge to restore 13 million hectares of degraded and deforested land by 2020.
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on India's international environmental commitments and land restoration targets.

claims.claimText: The Environment Audit (EA) Rules, 2025 were notified by the MoEFCC under the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: MoEFCC notified the Environment Audit (EA) Rules, 2025 under the Environment Protection Act (1986).
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on the parent legislation of the EA Rules.
distractorHooks: Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981

claims.evidenceExcerpt: EA differs from EIA as EIA is conducted prior to project establishment, while EA checks conformity to the Environmental Management Plan.

claims.claimText: The Environment Audit (EA) Rules, 2025 feature a Two-Tiered System comprising a government regulator review by CPCB/SPCB (Tier-1) and an Environment auditor-based mechanism (Tier-2).
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Features a Two-Tiered System: Tier-1 (CPCB/SPCB review) and Tier-2 (Environment auditor-based mechanism).
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on the structural tiers of the EA Rules.

claims.claimText: The Environment Audit Designate Agency (EADA) is responsible for the certification, registration, and oversight of Environment Auditors.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Environment Audit Designate Agency (EADA) is responsible for certification, registration, and oversight of Environment Auditors.
claims.examUse: Correct-pair question matching the agency with its function.

claims.claimText: Environmental Auditing was initially introduced in India in 1992 under the Environment (Protection) Rules, requiring the submission of annual environmental statements to State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs).
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Introduced initially in 1992 under the Environment (Protection) Rules, requiring annual environmental statements to SPCBs.

claims.sectionTitle: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and Ex-Post Facto Clearance
claims.claimText: An ex-post facto environmental clearance (EC) allows a project to continue operations even if it did not obtain an EC prior to the commencement of the project.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: An ex-post facto EC allows a project proponent to continue without obtaining an EC prior to project commencement.
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and implications of ex-post facto EC in environmental governance.
distractorHooks: Ex-post facto EC is granted only before project commencement
Ex-post facto EC strictly prohibits continuation of projects without prior clearance

claims.sectionTitle: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and Ex-Post Facto Clearance
claims.claimText: The Supreme Court overturned its previous decision that had restrained the Centre from granting ex-post facto environmental clearance to mining projects.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: The Supreme Court reviewed and overturned its May 2025 decision in Vanashakti v. Union of India, which had restrained the Centre from granting ex-post facto environmental clearance (EC) to mining projects
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on recent judicial trends regarding EIA and mining projects.
distractorHooks: The Supreme Court completely banned ex-post facto ECs for mining projects
The Centre is constitutionally barred from granting ex-post facto ECs under all circumstances

claims.claimText: Under the EIA Notification 2006, Category A projects are handled by the MoEF&CC at the Central level, while Category B projects are handled by the SEIAA at the State level.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Projects are categorized into Category A (Central level, MoEF&CC) and Category B (State level, SEIAA).
claims.examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question swapping the authorities for Category A and Category B projects.
distractorHooks: Swapping MoEF&CC and SEIAA
Claiming both are handled by the Central Government
Claiming Category A is handled by NGT

claims.claimText: The Supreme Court ruled in the Common Cause v. UoI (2017) case that ex post facto or retrospective environmental clearance is invalid and alien to environmental jurisprudence.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: Supreme Court held in Common Cause v. UoI (2017) that ex post facto environmental clearance is invalid.
claims.examUse: Statement-based question on whether retrospective environmental clearance is legally permissible in India.
distractorHooks: Stating ex post facto clearance is allowed with a penalty

claims.claimText: The EIA Notification 2006 mandates prior Environmental Clearance for over 39 listed activities.
claims.evidenceExcerpt: EIA Notification 2006 lists over 39 activities requiring prior EC.
claims.examUse: Statement-based question testing if prior clearance is mandatory and the governing notification.
distractorHooks: Stating clearance can be obtained after project commencement
Citing the Environment Protection Act 1986 directly instead of the EIA Notification 2006

claims.sectionTitle: Great Nicobar Island Project (GNIP) and Tribal Rights
claims.claimText: The Great Nicobar Island Project (GNIP) was conceived by NITI Aayog, with the Andaman and Nicobar Islands Integrated Development Corporation (ANIIDCO) serving as its nodal agency.

claims.sectionTitle: Great Nicobar Island Project (GNIP) and Tribal Rights
claims.claimText: The administration in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands has cited the Protection of Aboriginal Tribes Act, 1956 (PAT) to bypass the Forest Rights Act, 2006 (FRA) mandate requiring Gram Sabha consent for forest land diversion.

distractorHooks: Gram Sabha consent is mandatory and cannot be bypassed under any law in A&N Islands

claims.sectionTitle: Great Nicobar Island Project (GNIP) and Tribal Rights
